thesis: Micron is the only U.S.-based memory manufacturer and a key supplier of HBM3E for AI accelerators. Q2 FY2026 revenue hit a record $23.86B (+84% YoY) with gross margins expanding to ~81%. The AI memory supercycle is real, but the stock has risen ~750% from 52-week lows and trades at ~49x TTM P/E, leaving little room for error in a notoriously cyclical industry.

drivers: HBM demand exceeds supply, ASPs rise further
Data center memory spend sustains 50%+ growth
HBM4 qualification wins lock in multi-year supply agreements

risks: Inventory builds as AI capex slows
Samsung and SK hynix intensify HBM competition

base_case.assumptions: AI memory demand remains strong but moderates in H2 2027. FY2026 revenue of $85–90B with EPS of $35–40. Cyclical normalization begins but is cushioned by HBM mix shift.

catalysts.event: Q4 FY2026 earnings and FY2027 guidance
NVIDIA GTC / next-gen AI accelerator announcements

key_risks: Intense competition from Samsung and SK hynix
High valuation leaves little margin for error
